title: Sign-out does not clear the private address-labels SWR cache
reasoning: Address labels are explicitly treated as private data, but sign-out tries to clear only array-shaped SWR keys whose first element is "address-labels". The actual labels cache key is the string "address-labels:all", so this mutate predicate never matches it. In a shared-browser or account-switch flow, SWR can keep the previous user's labels under that key and expose them immediately when the next authenticated session mounts before the fresh /api/address-labels response replaces the cache.
recommendation: Clear the exact string key on sign-out, or change the predicate to match both the string key and any future address-label key namespace, for example `key === "address-labels:all" || (Array.isArray(key) && key[0] === "address-labels")`. Prefer exporting the key/helper from the provider or a shared module so sign-out and provider cannot drift again.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included AuthStatus tests cover sign-in callback URL behavior only. They never render an authenticated session, click Sign out, or assert that the mutate predicate matches the provider's actual SWR key.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an AuthStatus sign-out test that renders an authenticated session, clicks Sign out, captures the predicate passed to `mutate`, and asserts it returns true for `"address-labels:all"`. A provider-level account-switch test with seeded SWR cache would be even stronger.
minimumFixScope: Update AuthStatus cache invalidation and add a focused sign-out regression test.

history.note: Sign-out now clears the exact address-labels SWR string key and namespace array keys via shared predicate; regression added.
